political.s: Employee donations from Levi Strauss heavily favor Democratic candidates and causes. Between 2019-2020, employees contributed $487,171 total, with $477,421 going to Democratic Party. The Levi Strauss Foundation explicitly funds left-of-center causes including reproductive rights, immigrant advocacy, and voting access initiatives.

charity.s: Levi Strauss Foundation has invested $397 million since 1952 in left-of-center causes. Foundation focuses on democracy, immigrant rights, reproductive justice, and worker rights. Annual giving through foundation averages $9-10 million with additional employee matching programs and Red Tab Foundation providing $2.4 million annually to employees.
charity.focus: Democracy, immigrant rights, reproductive justice, worker rights

environment.s: Levi Strauss committed to net-zero emissions by 2050 with science-based targets validated by SBTi. Goals include 90% GHG reduction in owned facilities by 2025 and 42% supply chain reduction by 2030. Uses sustainable cotton sourcing (83% from sustainable sources in 2020) and innovative techniques like waterless finishing. Mixed independent verification challenges performance claims.

labor.s: Pioneer in supplier code of conduct (1991) with comprehensive workplace standards banning forced labor and child labor. Worker Well-Being initiative reached 219,000+ workers. However, recent Amsterdam lawsuit alleged misleading claims about Turkish supplier working conditions. Fashion Transparency Index scored 60% in 2023, indicating incomplete living wage implementation and limited third-party certification.

dei.s: Strong DEI commitment with 99% shareholder support in April 2025 rejecting anti-DEI proposal. Company reports gender pay parity and has 82% inclusion score. However, 2020 assessment found women underrepresented in senior roles and Latinx/Hispanic and Black employees underrepresented. Established Chief Diversity Officer position and launched Diversity Action Plans.

animals.s: Not applicable - apparel/textile company focused on denim and clothing products with no animal testing or testing operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - apparel company does not manufacture or sell firearms or gun-related products.

privacy.s: Privacy policy permits third-party advertising companies to collect browsing data across unrelated websites and combine with device/household data for targeted advertising. Company permits interest-based advertising and tracking technologies. Policy last updated September 2019, somewhat outdated. Complies with state-level privacy laws but allows extensive data sharing with business partners.

execPay.s: CEO Michelle Gass earned $13.70M total compensation in 2024 (11.2% salary, 88.8% stock/bonuses). Above average for similar-sized companies ($7.70M median). Top executives averaged $6M annually (2016-2021). Executive compensation consistent with company performance but represents significant multiple over median employee salary of approximately $123,634.
execPay.ratio: 111:1 (estimated based on CEO vs median employee)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
